source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design a 1-meter cylindrical airframe tube section of a shoulder-launched anti-UAV missile (MANPADS-class UAS interceptor) benchmark as a multi-part co-bonded assembly of: the filament-wound CFRP barrel tube, four circumferentially distributed fin-mount lug reinforcement patches bonded at 90 deg intervals on the tube, a forward nose-interface ring that carries the IR seeker dome, and an aft motor-interface ring that carries the nozzle. Total missile launch mass <= 15 kg (incl. warhead + motor + seeker + airframe), tube outer diameter 100 mm, engagement slant range 4000 m, peak acceleration at motor burnout 40 g axial, maneuver capability 20 g lateral at Mach 2.0 terminal engagement, IR seeker dome forward of the frame, launcher tube inner diameter 105 mm. Material: filament-wound carbon/epoxy composite cylinder [+/-45/0/90]s layup, quasi-isotropic effective E1=E2=55 GPa, G12=20 GPa, Poisson 0.30, density 1550 kg/m^3, tensile strength 800 MPa, compressive 600 MPa, through-thickness thermal conductivity 0.5 W/mK. Tube wall nominal 3 mm, reinforced at four hardpoints where fin-mount lugs interface. Tube must survive aerodynamic skin heating of 250 C at Mach 2.0 over a 6-second powered fly-out. Load cases: (LC1) launch eject + burnout: 40 g axial on 15 kg mass = 5886 N axial distributed through tube; (LC2) terminal maneuver: 20 g lateral on 10 kg post-burn mass = 1962 N lateral applied at the CG 0.6 m aft of nose; (LC3) aerodynamic pressure: external q = 250 kPa at Mach 2.0 sea level terminal phase, equivalent to 79 kN distributed across 1 m tube length; (LC4) thermal: 250 C sustained skin temperature, 40 C internal initial; (LC5) launch tube seal: 200 kPa internal over-pressure from motor start before nozzle clearance; (LC6) fin-lug point load: 500 N lateral at each fin lug during maneuver. Pass/fail: (R1) max principal strain in ply below 0.75% (conservative fracture limit for thermally-degraded CFRP at 250 C); (R2) max through-thickness shear below 40 MPa; (R3) launcher tube clearance (OD + thermal expansion + ovalization) below 104.5 mm inside 105 mm launcher; (R4) first ovalization mode >= 200 Hz; (R5) tube mass <= 1.2 kg per meter; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue under LC3 external pressure >= 2.5. Verification is performed on the assembled tube + lug + end-ring multi-body model with representative co-bonded interface stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP + layup table, coupled thermal-structural FEA per case, modal, buckling, and mass report.
short_prompt:
Design a 1 m cylindrical airframe tube section of a shoulder-launched anti-UAV missile (MANPADS-class UAS interceptor) benchmark as a multi-part co-bonded assembly.

Total missile launch mass <= 15 kg, tube OD 100 mm, launcher tube ID 105 mm, slant range 4000 m, peak acceleration at motor burnout 40 g axial, maneuver 20 g lateral at Mach 2.0 terminal. Filament-wound carbon/epoxy [+/-45/0/90]s (E1=E2=55 GPa, G12=20 GPa, Poisson 0.30, density 1550 kg/m^3, tensile 800 MPa, compressive 600 MPa, k 0.5 W/mK), nominal 3 mm wall. Aerodynamic skin heating 250 C at Mach 2.0 over a 6-second fly-out.

Load cases.
- LC1 launch eject + burnout: 40 g axial on 15 kg = 5886 N axial through tube.
- LC2 terminal maneuver: 20 g lateral on 10 kg post-burn mass = 1962 N at CG 0.6 m aft of nose.
- LC3 aerodynamic q = 250 kPa at Mach 2.0 sea level = 79 kN distributed over 1 m.
- LC4 thermal: 250 C sustained skin, 40 C internal initial.
- LC5 launch-tube seal: 200 kPa internal over-pressure from motor start.
- LC6 fin-lug point load: 500 N lateral at each fin lug.

Launcher OD clearance under thermal + ovalization below 104.5 mm inside 105 mm; tube mass <= 1.2 kg/m; buckling eigenvalue under LC3 >= 2.5.

prompt.title: MANPADS-class airframe tube (multi-part co-bonded assembly)
prompt.brief: CFRP filament-wound 100 mm OD airframe tube + 4 fin-lug reinforcement patches + forward nose ring + aft motor ring, Mach 2 terminal, 40 g burnout.

parts.name: airframe_barrel_tube
parts.role: filament-wound [+/-45/0/90]s CFRP cylindrical barrel carrying axial + external-pressure + thermal loads

parts.name: fin_lug_reinforcement_patch
parts.role: one of four bonded local reinforcement pads on the tube OD where fin-mount lugs attach (LC6)

parts.name: forward_nose_interface_ring
parts.role: forward end-ring interfacing to the IR seeker dome assembly

parts.name: aft_motor_interface_ring
parts.role: aft end-ring interfacing to the rocket motor case and carrying the nozzle seal

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +Z forward along missile axis, fin lugs at 0, 90, 180, 270 deg

boundary_conditions.interfaces: fin-lug reinforcement patches co-bonded to tube OD with representative bonded interface stiffness; nose ring and motor ring bonded + mechanically indexed to tube ends
boundary_conditions.support: launcher-tube ID support during LC1/LC5; free-free for modal

prompt.objective: minimize tube mass per meter while passing ply strain, buckling, thermal clearance, and modal gates

interfaces.load_path: fin-lug reinforcement patch co-bonded to tube OD
forward nose ring bonded + indexed to tube forward end
aft motor ring bonded + sealed to tube aft end

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The airframe tube is explicitly reinforced at four fin-mount hardpoints and carries distinct forward/aft end interfaces for the IR seeker and motor case; LC6 fin-lug point loads cross the bonded reinforcement interface and must be modelled as a multi-body co-bonded structure.
notes.speculation_caveats: Nose-ring and motor-ring materials and fin-lug patch layout are speculative; tube material, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
